title: viscum cuneifolium var. lanceolatum
intro: Viscum cuneifolium var. lanceolatum, commonly known as mistletoe, is a semi-parasitic plant that grows on the branches of its host tree, obtaining its nutrients while still providing some photosynthesis. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Viscum cuneifolium var. lanceolatum include its potential to boost the immune system, reduce stress and anxiety, and exhibit antitumor activity. Herbal preparations of mistletoe can be found in the form of extracts, tinctures, and dried leaves, which are often used in teas, infusions, and as a supplement to support overall health.
